title: The Marketplace of Renamed Things

features.name: vendor-stand-files
features.desc-on-touch: A marketplace stall selling renamed versions of things. The archive sent files here expecting them to be catalogued. Instead, they were renamed by use, by resemblance, by the villagers who touched them. The original names are fading from the tags.

features.name: price-tag-glyph
features.desc-on-touch: What was written there originally. The second name, written over it. A third in the margin. Each one marks a moment when meaning shifted. The archive stopped trying to fix them. Some files have five names now.

features.name: dusk-banner-arch
features.desc-on-touch: An archway made of sunset-coloured cloth, announcing tonight's trading. The sign announcing what names are being offered today. Every day it changes. The archive tried to keep an archive of archives—a list of what was renamed. The list is now longer than the original catalogue.

features.name: trader-sprite
features.desc-on-touch: A figure moving between stalls, carrying a ledger of renames. It doesn't speak but writes constantly. The archive hired it to keep track of meaning-shifts. Instead, it became part of the market. Now it renames itself hourly.

features.name: exchange-pool
features.desc-on-touch: Water where traders have begun washing their renamed goods, hoping the original names will surface from the water. They never do. The pool is copper-coloured now from all the tags and coins dropped in. A wish-archive of its own.

features.name: corruption-stack
features.desc-on-touch: The archive's own attempt to organize the renames: stacked by date, by frequency of use, by colour of the ink used for each renaming. Every system it tried failed. The stack is taller than the stalls now. It has become an unread monument.

inscription: the files arrive by water and the village renames them by resemblance; the archive now keeps both names and the pain between them

file.name: Market_Rename_Index_CORRUPTED_1970s-Present.md
file.note: An attempt to track name-mutations. Now contains poetry fragments that may have arrived from another archive's signal—references to an endless surgery on a beach, names changing during procedures, sal

exits.label: back toward the reading room where names still mean one thing
into the village square where more renames begin
toward the river source where names are still being born
into the deeper marketplace where older renames are still being traded
